response:
The fare $\eta$ equals 10 when the distance $\xi$ is at most 4 kilometers. If the distance exceeds 4 kilometers then the fare becomes 10 plus 2 times the amount of kilometers over 4. This calculation rounds up any fraction of a kilometer to the next whole number before adding the extra charge. The spring season brings many travelers to the airport for their journeys.

We find that a fare of 38 yuan implies a total calculated distance of 19 kilometers for the trip. Since the actual road distance is 15 kilometers the extra 4 kilometers must come from waiting time. Each minute of waiting counts as 0.2 kilometers so 20 minutes of stopping creates exactly 4 kilometers of virtual distance. The spring weather often causes traffic delays that increase the total fare for passengers. A maximum of 20 minutes represents the longest possible stop time for this specific fare amount.
